I'll just tell him I'm nutritionist a couple of the other people on on our staff are nutritionists So that's how we develop the meal plan is between us um People at PCOS you have to manage insulin heavily That Right, well, that's what I'm about to explain. So you're basically resistant to insulin more than a normal person So what we have to do is manage the glycemic index of your carbohydrates So certain carbohydrates, um Have a high glycemic index which is going to cause a huge insulin spike which someone in your situation very bad So we keep it a low glycemic index carbohydrates So beans would be a good option for that because they are low glycemic index And then potato would be a good option if you boil it not bake it when you bake a potato Sweet potatoes fine, too, but you have to boil it. Oh, yeah Because the glycemic index goes up if you bake it, but it stays down if you boil it Yeah, and then adding fiber and every single meal that you have so vegetables and then adding a healthy fat in every single meal And then even adding Yeah, or I mean there's a ton of other good ones, but yeah like avocado All those things help lower the glycemic index of carbohydrates Which is then going to help you manage your insulin better and that's how you help That's how we still get people like you to lose fat Yes I'm sorry if it's like like too much or from How much weight have you lost like in the last let's say 60 days Okay The last time I was trying because before before Hawaii I just went on 35.
